title: SCBE Coding-Primary 7B QLoRA v6c (DRAFT — coding-output-primary mix)
description: Scale-up targeted at agent coding quality. Coding-related shards weighted ~93% of training rows. Sacred-Tongue conlang lane retained via bijective_codeflow which IS the cross-tongue code translation shard. DRAFT — DO NOT DISPATCH WITHOUT USER REVIEW.
system_prompt: You are an SCBE-AETHERMOORE coding agent. Code is the primary output. The Sacred Tongues map bijectively to code languages (KO=Python, AV=JavaScript, RU=Rust, CA=Mathematica, UM=Haskell, DR=Markdown). Preserve slot alignment when translating across tongues. Emit minimal, executable code. Use governance verdicts (PASS/QUARANTINE/ESCALATE/DENY) only when the prompt is a verification task.
notes: DRAFT — DO NOT DISPATCH WITHOUT USER REVIEW. Cost estimate: l4x1 x 120 min ~= $3-6 on HF Pro.
Train mix (1,699 rows total): 936 bijective_codeflow + 530 CA/GeoSeal repair + 163 coding-specific + 32 foundation_bundle + 72 aligned_foundations OOD = 93% coding-related, 7% drift guard.
Eval mix (220 holdout rows): 104 bijective + 13 CA/GeoSeal + 70 coding-specific + 10 foundation + 23 OOD anchor.
max_seq_length raised to 768 (vs v6's 512) because bijective_codeflow rows include full Python+JS+Rust source blocks side-by-side; many rows will truncate at 512.

Eval contract authored 2026-05-05 at config/model_training/coding_verification_eval_contract.json — coding_verification_unseen_eval_v1. 12 prompts spanning bijective translation (count_vowels KO->UM, multi-lens triple), body fidelity (inventory_unique seen-set guard, safe_subtract None-guard, clamp_value, merge_counts, first_positive Option), approval-card verdict, KO-route running_average, and code/chemistry lane boundary. Thresholds: minimum_pass_rate 0.7 with 3 must_pass items. Forbidden tokens word-boundary-matched per double_blind_training_eval._contains_forbidden.

push_adapter remains FALSE for v6c first dispatch — flip to true only after a base-model dry-run shows the contract correctly distinguishes a verbose-ideal candidate from a skeleton-only candidate, AND v6c training round actually clears the gate.

Components added 2026-05-04 (H-JEPA, golden-helix, spine overlay, council dispatch) intentionally NOT included here. v6c isolates the data-mix-and-base-scale variable for clean attribution. Layer those in v6d after v6c baseline is measured.

Comparison plan: v5 (0.5B chem) vs v6c (7B coding-primary) on bijective_codeflow holdout + coding_approval_metrics eval. Expected lift on 7B base alone: substantial. Expected lift from coding-primary mix: substantial. Combined lift should be measurable above v5 baseline.
